suite: EPIC 2E-J ENV-B1B PART 11 — In-Memory App Harness static/functional self-test

results.test: 1. scanCodeMask marks the real import statement as code, and the comment/JSDoc/string mentions as non-code
1b. a real import statement is still correctly found even when an earlier same-line-terminator-free comment also contains the word "import ... from"
2. static "import ... from" specifiers (default+named, namespace) are rewritten to canonical IDs, quotes preserved
results.evidence:
import Foo, { bar, baz as qux } from 'https://lumixa.invalid/ui/x.js';
import * as ns from 'https://lumixa.invalid/y.js';

results.test: 3. side-effect "import '...';" (no clause, no "from") is rewritten to its canonical ID
4. export-from forms (named, *, * as ns) all rewrite their specifiers to canonical IDs
5. a literal dynamic import('...') is rewritten to its canonical ID
6. a non-literal dynamic import (a variable, not a single string literal) is honestly rejected, never rewritten or guessed at
results.evidence: {"edges":[],"rejected":[{"specifier":null,"reason":"non-literal dynamic import rejected (argument is not a single string literal)","context":"dynamic-import","raw":"path"}]}

results.test: 6b. a concatenated dynamic import argument is also rejected as non-literal (not just a bare variable)
results.evidence: {"edges":[],"rejected":[{"specifier":null,"reason":"non-literal dynamic import rejected (argument is not a single string literal)","context":"dynamic-import","raw":"'./a.js' + '.js'"}]}

results.test: 7/8. traversal, backslash, node:, bare-package, and absolute-URL specifiers are all honestly rejected (never rewritten, never silently dropped without a reason)
results.evidence: [{"snippet":"import x from '../../../etc/passwd';","rejectedReason":"module specifier escapes project root: \"../../../etc/passwd\" from \"\""},{"snippet":"import x from '../outside.js';","rejectedReason":"module specifier escapes project root: \"../outside.js\" from \"\""},{"snippet":"import x from 'C:\\\\windows\\\\evil.js';","rejectedReason":"bare package import not supported: \"C:\\\\windows\\\\evil.js\""},{"snippet":"import x from 'node:fs';","rejectedReason":"node: import not allowed in a Browser module: \"node:fs\""},{"snippet":"import x from 'left-pad';","rejectedReason":"bare package import not supported: \"left-pad\""},{"snippet":"import x from 'https://evil.example.com/x.js';","rejectedReason":"absolute URL / protocol-relative import not allowed (network-zero contract): \"https://evil.example.com/x.js\""}]

results.test: 7b. resolveModuleSpecifierToProjectPath rejects escape-the-root relative paths and correctly resolves root-absolute ("/core/...") paths regardless of importer depth
9. a module imported from multiple places is stored once in the graph (module reuse, not a duplicate), with zero duplicateCanonicalIds reported
The real project builds successfully into an in-memory app (no exception)
10. the Import Map <script type="importmap"> appears before the first <script type="module"> tag
11. no <script type="module" src="...​"> tag remains in the document (the ui/app.js entry was converted to an inline canonical import)
11b. the app.js entry point is present as an inline canonical import
The Import Map is valid, parseable JSON with an "imports" object
Every Import Map value is a data:text/javascript;base64,... URL (never a network URL)
Every Import Map key is a canonical https://lumixa.invalid/... identifier (never navigated to)
toEvidenceSummary() never includes full module source text or data: URLs (Part 10 redaction requirement)
Google Fonts network dependency is fully removed from the in-memory document
fontFallbackUsed is derived from an actual removed-link count, not a hardcoded literal disconnected from any check
12/19/20. Building the in-memory app twice never modifies index.html, ui/app.js, or any Core/Mapping/XMP file on disk (Production, Mapping, and XMP remain byte-identical)
10a. probeStorageAccess reports SecurityError (name only) on an opaque-origin window, before any install — reproduces the real about:blank failure mode
10b. compatibility layer install eliminates the SecurityError — native accessible Storage is never what gets replaced (this path only ever runs when access already failed)
10c. probeStorageAccess reports NATIVE_STORAGE_AVAILABLE-equivalent (both accessible) when Storage already works — this is the condition under which the smoke test never installs the layer
10d. localStorage and sessionStorage use separate stores (functional, not just structural)
10e. methods live on Storage.prototype (getItem/setItem/removeItem/clear/key), not as own properties on the instances
10f. objects do not define own setItem/removeItem/clear that would bypass Storage.prototype (re-verified directly, not only inside runFullStorageVerification)
10g. values (and keys) are String-coerced
10h. getItem returns null for a missing key
10i. exact Reference restoration is checked (strict === on Storage.prototype.setItem after wrap+restore)
10j. runFullStorageVerification (the exact function invoked in-Browser) passes all A-F + PART 4 checks against the fake opaque-origin window
10k. the Browser-invocation sources are self-contained (bind only to window/Storage, capture no outer-scope module state)
qa/helpers/playwright-opaque-origin-storage.mjs is readable
10l. no fs write anywhere in the Storage compatibility helper (it never imports node:fs at all — pure in-memory logic)
10m. no Network use anywhere in the Storage compatibility helper, and it never imports "playwright" itself
10n. outside of comments, the Storage compatibility helper never references any core/, ui/, or index.html path (it only ever touches window/Storage objects passed in by the caller)
10o. source order: the compatibility layer is installed AFTER the about:blank navigation and BEFORE page.setContent (matches the required PART 5 sequence)
10p. when native Storage is already accessible, the smoke test never calls the installer (native accessible Storage is not replaced)
10q. the in-memory smoke test never uses the legacy NOT_RUN_ENVIRONMENT_BLOCKED decision (retired now that the in-memory Browser path is available)
10r. a second, independent BrowserContext is created and checked for Storage isolation (PART 6 zero-persistence contract)
10s. App storage observation records key NAMES only (store.key(i)), never calls getItem to read values into the result
qa/helpers/playwright-in-memory-app.mjs is readable
qa/playwright-in-memory-app-smoke.mjs is readable
The in-memory app helper never imports "playwright" itself — statically testable even when the package is unavailable
The in-memory app helper never writes to disk (read-only: readFile only) — cannot mutate Production/Mapping/XMP files
12. no local HTTP server is constructed anywhere in this harness (no http.createServer / .listen(port))
13. page.goto() is never called with anything except the ABOUT_BLANK_URL constant ("about:blank?qa=1") — no virtual-origin, localhost, file:, or data: navigation anywhere in this harness
The smoke test loads the application via page.setContent(), not via any Browser navigation
The smoke test creates its BrowserContext with serviceWorkers: "block" and launches Chromium with the required sandbox args
The smoke test never attempts to download a Browser
This new harness does not modify or depend on the existing Virtual-Origin helper's routing installer (kept separate per spec)
The in-memory app helper reuses the existing Virtual-Origin helper's proven path-safety primitives (resolveSafeLocalPath) rather than re-implementing traversal defenses from scratch
Every record() call in the new smoke test passes a PASS/FAIL/NOT_TESTED string (learned from the ENV-B1A-R Part 12 fix — never repeat the boolean-record bug in new code)
FIX 8 (ENV-B2-F1): the smoke test no longer uses the old brittle `results.filter(FAIL).length===0` decision pattern, and instead imports + calls the canonical computeInMemoryHarnessDecision(results)
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — non-empty, all-PASS well-formed rows — expected PASS_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS_READY
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — empty Array — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a row missing test — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a row with a blank test — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a row missing result — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a boolean result row — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a NOT_TESTED row — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — a genuine FAIL row — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
FIX 11 (ENV-B2-F2): computeInMemoryHarnessDecision() — an unknown status row — expected FAIL_IN_MEMORY_HARNESS
17/18. Neither new file ever references or regenerates the existing Browser results file or the Final Phase C results file

disclaimer: This suite calls the helper's real exported functions directly against synthetic snippets and the real project files, and audits both new harness files' source text plus Production-file hash immutability. It does not launch a Browser and proves nothing about actual Playwright/Chromium runtime behavior — that is the separate runtime smoke test's job.
